subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: triangulated across multilateral bodies (IMF Article IV, OECD, World Bank, IDB, World Justice Project, RSF, GI-TOC) + Costa Rican official sources (BCCR, INEC, OIJ, TSE) + Costa Rican/regional press (Tico Times, La Nación).

Source-lean: election/crime/press detail leaned heavily on the English-language Tico Times and US outlets; Spanish-language La Nación/Semanario Universidad/CRHoy were not read directly, so press-freedom and political nuance carry some Anglophone framing risk.
Rising narco-transit violence is the standout drag on an otherwise strong, demilitarized democracy.

economic.notes: Credible macro management (fiscal reform plus an inflation-targeting central bank) and a diversified, resilient export sector; persistent deflation a mild nuance.

fiscal_state.notes: A 2018 reform plus an IMF program drove debt below 60% GDP with a primary surplus (~1.3%) and a falling deficit; debt projected under 55% by 2030. (IMF 2025/2026 Article IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The BCCR is a credible inflation-targeter (cut the policy rate from 9.0% to ~3.25%); now fighting deflation, undershooting target. (BCCR, IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The colón is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: 2025 closed at ~-1.2% and early 2026 near -2.5% (deepest deflation in decades); price stability is excellent but a persistent sub-target undershoot is a mild credibility concern. (BCCR/INEC)

banking_stability.notes: The system stayed solvent and liquid through recent shocks; the BCCR flags consumer over-indebtedness and ~27% loan dollarization as structural risks. (BCCR financial-stability report)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Diversified, resilient exports (~$19bn, +16%): medical devices now the top export, Intel reinvestment, a nearshoring tailwind, plus services/ecotourism; not a petrostate. A strong colón squeezes exporter margins (mild drag). (PROCOMER, Wilson Center)

institutional.notes: Solid democratic institutions and credible statistics, under populist strain and organized-crime pressure.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong by regional standards (independent judiciary and electoral tribunal), but checks and balances weakened under President Chaves and organized crime is pressuring institutions - a downward long-term pressure. (World Justice Project 2024/25)

statistical_integrity.notes: INEC, BCCR, and OIJ produce credible, transparent, independently cited data; OECD-member reporting standards. (OECD, IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A professional bureaucracy with strong public-health and electoral administration; weakened by a wage freeze and weak infrastructure delivery. (OECD Economic Survey 2025)

press_freedom.notes: Still among the freest in the region but the RSF rank slid for four straight years amid the president's verbal attacks and ad-withholding (the Constitutional Chamber ruled against him). (RSF 2025, CPJ)

political_social.notes: A robust democracy with a favorable, lawful residency pathway, against sharply elevated narco-transit violence and a new single-party legislative majority.

political_stability.notes: Feb 2026: Laura Fernández won with the first single-party Assembly majority since 1990, ending executive-legislative gridlock (stability up) but concentrating power around a populist project; peaceful transfer, high turnout. (TSE results)

civil_liberties.notes: Robust; rated 'Free,' with functioning courts protecting rights; mild erosion via press pressure. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful, durable, long-popular residency routes (pensionado, rentista, inversionista); the immigration-office backlog is the main friction. (DGME)

social_violence.notes: Homicide ~16.8/100k (near record), driven by cocaine-transit cartels; criminal organizations grew roughly tenfold in a decade; mostly gang-on-gang, with a slight 2025 dip but structurally elevated. (OIJ, GI-TOC Org Crime Index 2025)

demographics.notes: Still favorable but ageing fast; high life expectancy and falling fertility pressure the social-security/pension system long-term. (INEC, OECD)

geopolitical.notes: A demilitarized, stable, OECD-integrated democracy with no external conflict and an open capital account.

alliance_reliability.notes: No military since 1948, a stable US-aligned democracy and OECD member; a reliable partner. (OECD)

conflict_involvement.notes: No external conflicts and demilitarized; the only 'conflict' is internal narco-violence (scored under social_violence). (Freedom House)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open capital account, no controls, not sanctioned, OECD-integrated. (IMF AREAER, OECD)

physical_practical.notes: A renewable-energy and universal-healthcare leader, dragged by poor roads and rising crime atop real seismic/storm exposure.

climate.notes: The best-prepared Central American state, but dual-coast storms plus dry-corridor exposure. (WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: CCSS universal coverage with strong primary-care outcomes and high life expectancy; multi-month non-emergency waits; a strong private/medical-tourism tier. (PHCPI, World Bank, OECD)

infrastructure.notes: Poor roads (among the weakest in the OECD), congestion, and weak project delivery; decent telecom; IDB/CABEI-financed upgrades underway. (OECD, IDB)

crime_safety.notes: Rising violence concentrated in narco-corridors and specific neighborhoods; tourist/expat zones safer but petty crime is up. (OIJ, OSAC)

disaster_insurance.notes: High seismic (Ring of Fire) plus storm/flood exposure; the state insurer (INS) is functional; insurable but with a real catastrophe tail. (INS, USGS hazard context)

summary: A stable, demilitarized democracy with credible macro management (debt cut below 60% of GDP, an independent inflation-targeting central bank), a diversified and resilient export base (medical devices, semiconductors, ecotourism), universal healthcare, a renewable grid, and lawful long-popular residency routes. The drags are sharply rising narco-transit violence, poor road infrastructure, populist strain on institutions and the press, and real seismic/storm exposure.
